music.name: High Level feat. Kai Alce
music.why: Detroit deep-house record label veteran in a real listening room; richer than any club-circuit set this week
music.summary: Fatsouls Records presents High Level featuring Kai Alce (Detroit) at The Foundry SF on Saturday July 18, an intimate 8pm–2am deep house session from one of the genre's most respected selectors; 21+.

music.name: Craze x Dieselboy
music.why: Two genre veterans on the outdoor terrace; the SF drum-and-bass pick if Yaeji isn't your room
music.summary: DnB veterans Craze and Dieselboy headline The Midway's outdoor terrace on Friday July 17, with a jungle and drum-and-bass bill running 9pm to 2am; 21+.

music.name: Stephan Bodzin (Live)
music.why: Berlin producer doing a live set, not a DJ set — the distinction matters at this level of techno
music.summary: German techno producer Stephan Bodzin performs a full live set at 1015 Folsom in SF on Friday July 17, supported by Gru.di B3B Izik B3B Manu K and others across the multi-room venue; 21+, 10pm to 3am.

music.name: Uncle Waffles – The Midway 9-Year Anniversary
music.why: Dancing DJ viral star on The Midway's biggest night of the year — spectacle plus music, not just a DJ set
music.summary: Uncle Waffles headlines The Midway's 9th anniversary party on Saturday July 18, bringing her viral dancing-DJ amapiano and afrobeats set to the main room for a 21+ evening running 9pm to 2am.

music.name: Yaeji
music.why: Korean-Canadian cult DJ/producer who builds for the floor, not the blog — the most critically-interesting ticket this week
music.summary: Korean-Canadian DJ, producer and vocalist Yaeji headlines The Midway's Ride room on Friday July 17, blending house, leftfield club and K-pop-inflected dance music in a late-night 21+ set running 9pm to 2am.

music.name: Max Cooper – 3D/AV Live: Feeling Is Structure Tour [Fri]
music.why: Second night; same tour, different spatial mix — buy Thu or Fri, not both unless you go deep
music.summary: Max Cooper's 3D/AV Live returns to Gray Area for the second night of his Feeling Is Structure Tour on Friday July 17, 8pm to 11pm; each show's spatial configuration and light sculpture differs; 21+.

music.name: Lavern Day Party
music.why: Afternoon rooftop house before the club rush; catch it and still make an evening show
music.summary: All Day All Night Events presents Lavern for a progressive and tech house afternoon party at 620 Jones in SF on Saturday July 18, 2pm to 8pm; 21+.

music.name: Morgan Page: Classics Only
music.why: An entire night of era-defining big-room house from the producer who made many of those classics
music.summary: Morgan Page plays a classics-only DJ set at Audio SF on Friday July 17, spanning his catalog and the complete era of progressive and big-room house he helped shape; 21+.

music.name: Dirtybird Campout x Northern Nights Music Festival
music.why: Riverside redwoods camping rave with 30+ artists over 4 days; the full escape from the city this weekend
music.summary: Dirtybird Campout and Northern Nights unite for a joint 4-day festival at Cooks Valley Campground in Northern California redwoods, July 17–20, with CloZee, Walker & Royce b2b VNSSA, LP Giobbi, Ivy Lab, Mindchatter and 30+ more acts across multiple stages; 18+, camping required.

music.name: Beats On The Bay
music.why: Live electronic and drum and bass from a boat on SF Bay at golden hour — bucket-list Sunday pick
music.summary: A Place to Love presents Beats On The Bay, a Sunday evening yacht cruise on SF Bay from Alameda, July 19 5pm to 9:30pm, with live electronic, house and drum and bass; 21+.

music.name: Bob Moses & Cannons: Afterglow Tour
music.why: Amphitheater-scale live deep house under Berkeley hills stars; the premium production pick of the weekend
music.summary: Bob Moses and Cannons co-headline the Greek Theatre in Berkeley on July 17 for the Afterglow Tour, an outdoor summer evening of live deep house, indie electronic and vocal electronica starting at 7:30pm.

music.name: 115th Annual Hayes Valley Carnival
music.why: A block party with 115 years of neighborhood history; the all-ages SF street fair, not a ticketed event
music.summary: The 115th Annual Hayes Valley Carnival fills Hayes Street with free neighborhood energy on Saturday July 18, featuring Circus Bella performances, street games, prizes and local food vendors; all ages.

music.name: Bad Tuner, Veggi
music.why: Two producers bridging UK bass and leftfield house in a mid-size room; the low-key alternative to club techno
music.summary: Bad Tuner and Veggi co-headline The Independent SF on Friday July 17, exploring UK garage, funky house and downtempo bass for an 18+ crowd at 8:30pm.

music.name: Bass in the Burrow
music.why: Budget camping bass festival an hour south of SF; the approachable alternative to the Dirtybird splurge
music.summary: A three-day bass music camping festival near Santa Cruz, July 17–19 (Fri 5pm through Sun noon), with dubstep, drum and bass and house acts at an outdoor venue; 18+.

music.name: Club Crab
music.why: Six-dollar bass party in Japantown — the weekend's cheapest door, no irony intended
music.summary: Camillionaire presents Club Crab at Mr. Mahjong's in SF on Saturday July 18, a $6 miami bass, booty bass and dubstep party starting at 9pm; 21+.

music.name: Club Moniker: K Wata (live) + zi!
music.why: Live UK garage in the Loft while the main room runs something bigger; the niche crowd that knows exactly why it's here
music.summary: Club Moniker presents K Wata in a live set alongside zi! at Public Works Loft in SF on Friday July 17, spotlighting UK garage and club sound in the smaller room; 21+.

music.name: Death Guild
music.why: SF's oldest goth night — cheap cover, real darkness, no tourist traps
music.summary: Death Guild, San Francisco's original goth/industrial weekly since the '90s, takes over DNA Lounge on Monday July 13, with DJs Decay, Joe Radio and Melting Girl in the main room and Sage/Bit in the lounge, 9:30pm to 2:30am, 18+.

music.name: Droid9 pres. Alex O'Rion
music.why: Droid9 label night in the Loft — underground prog crowd, not the main-room spectacle one floor down
music.summary: Droid9 Presents brings Alex O'Rion for a progressive and melodic house night at Public Works Loft in SF on Saturday July 18, 9pm to 2am; 21+.

music.name: Dvirnuns at Madarae
music.why: Slick SoMa club running indie-house — the quieter alternative when the warehouse scene feels like too much
music.summary: Dvirnuns headlines an indie dance and progressive house night at Madarae SF on Saturday July 18, 9pm to 3am, with free RSVP before 11pm; 21+.

music.name: Five Finger Disco x Elements
music.why: Outdoor afternoon funk in West Oakland; queer-welcoming and sweaty, not a rooftop Instagram moment
music.summary: Five Finger Disco and Elements present a funky house and soulful disco outdoor afternoon at 7th West in Oakland, Saturday July 18, 3pm to 9pm; 21+.

music.name: Fuck The System 4: Variance Krew & Birdhouse
music.address: 599 Cesar Chavez St, San Francisco, CA 94124
music.why: Afternoon bass slam at a warehouse venue from the local DIY crew; raw energy over production polish
music.summary: Variance Krew and Birdhouse present Fuck The System 4, a riddim, tearout and dubstep showcase at 3rd & Army in SF on Saturday July 18 from 3pm; 18+.

music.name: Hubba Hubba Revue: Goth vs. Emo
music.why: DNA's monthly burlesque spectacle with a goth-vs-emo costume theme; theatrical and campy, not a standard club night
music.summary: Hubba Hubba Revue returns to DNA Lounge for a Goth vs Emo edition on Sunday July 19, with burlesque, comedy and themed performances; doors 7:30pm, 18+.

music.name: Japan Day 2026
music.address: Post St & Laguna St, San Francisco, CA 94115
music.why: Free Japantown outdoor stage with J-pop and cultural acts — the zero-cost Sunday option for the family
music.summary: Japan Day 2026 fills Japantown's outdoor stage with J-pop performances and Japanese arts and culture for a free public festival noon to 5pm on Sunday July 19; all ages, Post & Laguna.

music.name: K?D – End of the Universe Tour (Final Future Bass Show)
music.why: The artist's self-declared last future bass show — one of those rare see-it-now moments before the era ends
music.summary: K?D concludes the End of the Universe Tour at Public Works SF on Saturday July 18, billing it as his final future bass show; 21+, 9pm to 2am.

music.name: Lana Del Rabies, Trace Amount, Insula Iscariot
music.why: Cult-tier industrial acts on a tiny Oakland stage; close and sweaty in a way no club night matches
music.summary: A three-band industrial, goth and darkwave showcase at Thee Stork Club in Oakland on Tuesday July 14, featuring underground favorites Lana Del Rabies, Trace Amount and Insula Iscariot in a close-quarters 21+ room.

music.name: Lindy in the Park – Swing Dance Party
music.address: JFK Drive, Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA 94118

music.name: Max Cooper – 3D/AV Live: Feeling Is Structure Tour [Thu]
music.why: Bespoke light-sculpture wraps the room in spatial techno — a science-art show, not a club experience
music.summary: Max Cooper brings his 3D/AV Live Feeling Is Structure Tour to Gray Area's immersive Grand Theater on Thursday July 16, with custom spatial audio and synchronized light architecture across the audience; 21+, standing, 7pm to 11pm.

music.name: Moontalk
music.why: Halcyon's tight floor running till 4am; the stripped-back late option after bigger shows close
music.summary: Moontalk takes over Halcyon SF on Saturday July 18 for a house-focused late-night session, 10pm to 4am; 21+.

music.name: Mura Masa // Curve
music.why: UK crossover producer at an intimate undisclosed SF venue — catch him before arena tours lock the format
music.summary: Mura Masa presents his Curve show at an undisclosed SF venue on Saturday July 18, blending hip-hop, electronic and synthpop in what 15Utah bills as an intimate production; 21+.

music.name: Pink Floyd – Wish You Were Here (Envelop Immersive Listening)
music.why: Spatial 32-speaker playback of a prog-rock masterpiece — ears-only, no phones, pure headspace
music.summary: Envelop SF plays Pink Floyd's Wish You Were Here in full 32-speaker 3D spatial audio in the intimate listening room at The Midway on Monday July 13, with sessions at 7:30pm and 9:30pm; 18+.

music.name: Playa Peace Project
music.why: Burning Man community fundraiser; the playa preview if you're heading to the desert next month
music.summary: Konnekted presents Playa Peace Project, a Burning Man fundraiser at The Loom in Oakland on Saturday July 18, 10pm to 3am, with house and indie dance music from community DJs; 21+.

music.name: Rjd2
music.why: The veteran behind Deadringer in a small bar — intimate compared to any arena bill this week
music.summary: Producer Rjd2 headlines Cornerstone in Berkeley on Friday July 17, delivering a live electronic and hip-hop instrumental performance starting at 9pm.

music.name: Samantha Loveridge
music.why: Rising name making her SF debut at Halcyon's tight floor; cheapest ticket of a stacked Friday night
music.summary: Samantha Loveridge makes her San Francisco debut at Halcyon SF on Friday July 17, blending afro, indie and melodic dance music for a 21+ late-night crowd; 10pm to 4am.

music.name: SF Bondage Ball: 25th Anniversary
music.why: 25 years of the Bay's most iconic fetish ball — 10 DJs on 4 floors, 30+ performers; SF underground's marquee night
music.summary: The SF Bondage Ball celebrates its 25th anniversary at DNA Lounge on Friday July 17, with 10 DJs across 4 dance floors, 30+ stage performers, live art and dungeon installations; 21+ and costumed.

music.name: Stern Grove Festival: Charley Crockett & Nicki Bluhm
music.address: 19th Ave & Sloat Blvd, San Francisco, CA 94132
music.why: Free picnic-blanket country in an old-growth eucalyptus grove — SF's best Sunday tradition for non-clubbers
music.summary: Stern Grove Festival presents Charley Crockett and Nicki Bluhm for a free outdoor Sunday afternoon concert on July 19, with gates opening at noon and performance at 2pm in the historic Stern Grove amphitheater.

music.name: Sunset Rooftop Party with Ali Farahani
music.why: Downtown rooftop playa-tech house with bay views for under $25; relaxed end-of-weekend energy
music.summary: A sunset rooftop house party at the Via Hotel in SF's Mission Bay on Sunday July 19, 2pm to 7pm, with DJ Ali Farahani spinning playa tech, progressive and deep house; 21+.

music.name: Sunshine People Annual Cancer Birthday: Mike Dunn & Coflo
music.why: Chicago house OG Mike Dunn in an outdoor backyard — the real house-heads' daytime pick over any club option
music.summary: Sunshine People's annual Cancer Birthday party at Mad Oak's outdoor yard in Oakland, Saturday July 18 2pm–10pm, featuring Chicago house pioneer Mike Dunn and Coflo in a backyard daytime set; 21+.

music.name: Super Perreo – Latin Rave Day Party
music.why: Open-air Sunday perreo on The Midway terrace — reggaeton in the afternoon sun, no dress code
music.summary: Super Perreo presents a Latin rave day party on The Midway's outdoor terrace on Sunday July 19, 3pm to 8pm, with reggaeton and Latin EDM in the afternoon sun; 21+.

music.name: Texture Summit: Satoshi Tomiie, Philco, Jkey, Ledet
music.why: Japanese deep-house legend Satoshi Tomiie is rare in SF clubs; mid-size room with a real listening crowd
music.summary: Texture Summit presents Satoshi Tomiie (SushiTech, Japan) alongside Philco, Jkey and Ledet for a deep techno and house night at Monarch SF on Friday July 17, 9pm to 3am; 21+.

music.name: The Beach Boys – Pet Sounds (Envelop Immersive Listening)
music.why: Spatial surround treatment of one of rock's most harmonically layered studio albums
music.summary: Envelop SF presents The Beach Boys' Pet Sounds in their 32-speaker immersive playback system at The Midway on Tuesday July 14, with sessions at 7:30pm and 9:30pm; 18+.

music.name: Why 2K: 90s & 2000s Dance Party
music.why: Pure 90s-2000s crowd-pleaser at a proper venue; the fun-not-serious pick if camping isn't your move
music.summary: Why 2K brings DJ Ayo (Netwerk) for a night of 90s and 2000s pop, hip-hop, R&B and dance remixes at DNA Lounge on Saturday July 18, 10pm to 2:30am; 18+.

Marin Running Club: Thursday Trail Run
sports.address: 703 Tennessee Valley Rd, Mill Valley, CA 94941

sports.name: Marin Running Club: Tuesday Bunny Run

sports.address: Middle Dr W & Metson Rd, San Francisco, CA

sports.name: SFRRC Wednesday Fun Run in the Presidio
sports.address: 610 Old Mason St, San Francisco, CA 94129

sports.name: Sunset Dunes parkrun 5K
